title: Judgment reserved in ASIC case against IAG
full_text: Judgment reserved in ASIC case against IAG 04 May 2026 Federal Court hearings have concluded in the Australian Securities and Investments Commission case against IAG over home insurance loyalty discounts. Summing up was completed in Melbourne on Friday and Justice Stewart Anderson reserved judgment. ASIC has alleged Insurance Australia Ltd and the Insurance Manufacturers of Australia joint venture misled customers about discounts they would receive, because of the way pricing algorithms were used. IAG is contesting the allegations. The action relates to the SGIO, SGIC and RACV brands.
summary: The Federal Court has concluded hearings in the ASIC case against IAG regarding alleged misleading practices related to home insurance loyalty discounts. ASIC claims that IAG misled customers about the discounts due to the use of pricing algorithms. Judgment has been reserved by Justice Stewart Anderson.
publication:
